page: Amazon Web Services x8g.24xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. Built on the arm64 architecture, it features 96 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No local storage or dedicated GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance and memory throughput. The server achieves top-10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU testing, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, reaching top-tier status in both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the x8g.24xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound workloads, leveraging the power-efficient Graviton arm64 architecture to deliver strong multi-threaded performance per unit of resource. The primary hardware tradeoff is the complete absence of local NVMe storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage solutions. Additionally, while multi-core and memory-intensive tasks perform exceptionally well, workloads dependent on high single-core clock speeds or specialized x86 instruction sets will not benefit from this architecture.

This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive and heavily multi-threaded enterprise workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput static web serving, and complex software compilation. It is also capable of handling medium-to-large LLM inference text generation tasks, where it demonstrates top-tier performance in text generation speeds for 7B and 70B models.
description: The Amazon Web Services x8g.24xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server powered by a 96-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database and Redis throughput, though single-core performance remains average. Qualitatively, the arm64 architecture delivers excellent multi-threaded efficiency for memory-intensive applications. This server is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and medium-to-large LLM inference text generation.

1536.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB of RAM per core.
